ocr.text: The Drydocking and maintenance schedule for Yang Ming Marine from 2019 to 2024 reflects a systematic increase in
maintenance costs and an improvement in uptime ratio, critical for operational efficiency in the shipping and maritime sector. In
2019, the maintenance costs were $5M, with an uptime ratio of 92%, and a drydock frequency of 1.0. The following year, 2020,
saw an increase in maintenance costs to $6M and an uptime ratio rise to 93%, with a drydock frequency of 1.2. In 2021,
maintenance costs escalated to $7M, maintaining the upward trend with a continued increase in uptime ratio to 94% and a
drydock frequency of 1.3. The year 2022 recorded maintenance costs of $8M, further improvement in uptime ratio to 95%, and
a drydock frequency of 1.4. In 2023, the maintenance costs reached $9M, with a corresponding uptime ratio of 96% and a
The final year, 2024, projects maintenance costs to hit $10M, sustaining the trend with an anticipated uptime ratio of 97% and a
drydock frequency remaining at 1.5. The average cost over the period from 2019 to 2024 is calculated at $7.5M, with an
average uptime ratio of 94% and an average drydock frequency of 1.4 across the years. Overall, the total number of drydocks
scheduled during this period amounts to 5 drydocks, indicating a robust maintenance strategy aimed at enhancing vessel
reliability and performance in the competitive Asia-Pacific maritime landscape.
